title: Survival Analyses Protocol for Wolf Management and Livestock Protection
author: Francisco Santiago-Ávila
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.jsscnee
materials: ***Set data as survival time for PWP-GT model
*Graphing smoothed hazard estimates (hazard ratio) by group

steps.title: Variables and Sample STATA 14
steps.instruction: Variables and Sample STATA 14 .Do file used for survival analyses at each spatial scale

steps.title: Here, we make available basic sample...
steps.instruction: Here, we make available basic sample code for the statistical survival analyses employed in our manuscript. This code should allow for replication of analyses at all spatial scales for all datasets involved.*

steps.title: ID_TRS_yr --> identifier for each area-year...
steps.instruction: ID_TRS_yr --> identifier for each area-year combination (please see study for more information)

steps.title: Interv_dummy --> dummy variable for intervention...
steps.instruction: Interv_dummy --> dummy variable for intervention type (0 if ‘non-lethal’, 1 if ‘lethal’)

steps.title: Delay_in_yrstop_time --> time to recurrent event (in days) or censoring
steps.instruction: Delay_in_yrstop_time --> time to recurrent event (in days) or censoring

steps.title: status_event --> failure binary variable (1...
steps.instruction: status_event --> failure binary variable (1 if recurrent event; 0 if censored)

steps.title: yr_stratum --> order of recurrent events within a particular area-yr†
steps.instruction: yr_stratum --> order of recurrent events within a particular area-yr†

steps.title: *Testing equality of survival functions by group (Log-rank test)
steps.instruction: *Testing equality of survival functions by group (Log-rank test)

steps.title: *Adding time-varying covariate to account for proportional hazard assumption violation
steps.instruction: *Adding time-varying covariate to account for proportional hazard assumption violation

steps.title: ***Correlation between delay between incidents and...
steps.instruction: ***Correlation between delay between incidents and wolves killed (for ‘Lethal’ only)

steps.title: * This sample code does not...
steps.instruction: * This sample code does not include code lines for dropping or adding observations, which we did for the alternate datasets used. However, this should only involve basic coding, and descriptions of observations included or dropped for each dataset can be found in the study.

steps.title: † ‘Area’ refers to the section,...
steps.instruction: † ‘Area’ refers to the section, neighborhood or neighborhood of township area scales.

steps.title: ‡ A similar procedure was used...
steps.instruction: ‡ A similar procedure was used for the township and neighborhood of township scales, using their respective spatial unit identifiers.

steps.title: § This section was used for our main dataset
steps.instruction: § This section was used for our main dataset. We removed this section of code for our alternate ‘traps-placed’ dataset, to include as ‘lethal’ those cases were attempts were made to trap wolves (please see study).

steps.title: ** May need to break ties...
steps.instruction: ** May need to break ties in Delay_in_yrstop_time variable to retain observations (please see stset help in Stata)

steps.title: †† A separate dataset was created...
steps.instruction: †† A separate dataset was created including only those area-year records with strata (yr_stratum) values with enough observations to be properly modelled (please see study).
